title: AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast discusses whether AI agents should manage user passwords and detect compromised credentials, debates the implications of record vulnerability patches, and examines C-suite executives trading cybersecurity for innovation velocity. Panelists examine readiness of AI agents for passwordmanagement and trends in vulnerability management and risk tolerance.
writeup: This IBM Security Intelligence podcast episode debates whether AI agents are ready to manage user passwords and detect compromised credentials, alongside the security tradeoffs that raises for access control. The panel also examines a record-setting Patch Tuesday and broader trends in vulnerability management, plus C-suite decisions to trade cybersecurity caution for faster innovation. Key threads: the readiness of AI agents for password management, the systemic risk implications of record vulnerability patches, and shifting executive risk tolerance.
